---
title: "Analysis"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
#upload crash data then create new dataset which is just the number of crashes for a given day
setwd("~/Data205/Data-205-Capstone-Paul-Hanscom/data/external")
Crash <- read.csv("CleanedCrashData.csv")
Crash_Count <- Crash |>
  count(date, name = "count") 

#upload weather data then create new dataset of daily precipitation averages
setwd("~/Data205/Data-205-Capstone-Paul-Hanscom/data/processed")
Weather <- read.csv("CleanedWeatherData.csv")
Avg_Prcp <- Weather |>
  group_by(date) |>
  summarise(avg_prcp = mean(totalprcp)) |> 
  ungroup()   

#merge two new datasets based on date.
Crash_Prcp <- merge(Crash_Count, Avg_Prcp, by = "date")
```
```{r}
# graph average precipitation vs number of crashes
p1 <- ggplot(Crash_Prcp, aes(x = count,y = log10(avg_prcp)))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "red", linetype = "solid")+
  labs(
    title = "Number of Crashes Vs Log of Average Percipitation",
    x = "Number of Crashes",
    y = "Log Average Percipitiation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
p1
#save graph to github
setwd("~/Data205/Data-205-Capstone-Paul-Hanscom/analysis/images")
ggsave("AvgPrcp_vs_NumCrashes.png", plot = p1, width = 8, height = 6)
```
```{r}
#Conduct correlation test
result <- cor.test(Crash_Prcp$count, Crash_Prcp$avg_prcp)

print(result)
```
